verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 gives the bottom line, "lets a third-party app get access...without ever seeing their password"; B starts with a heading then similar claim.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to the core prescription and closes with the two common traps; B ends by offering "actual HTTP requests, or a code snippet," creating extra CTAs.
verdict.fabrication: B adds an unverified stronger claim: "OAuth 2.1 makes it mandatory"; A has no comparable unsupported escalation.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to save: it explains the wall, "access token is never exposed," the principle, front/back channel split, and the traps: "validating state" and "treating the authorization code as if it were the token."
